speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Fiddler, forbear. You grow too forward, sir.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Have you so soon forgot the entertainment

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Her sister Katherine welcomed you withal?

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: But, wrangling pedant, this is

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: The patroness of heavenly harmony.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Then give me leave to have prerogative,

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: And when in music we have spent an hour,

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Your lecture shall have leisure for as much.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Preposterous ass, that never read so far

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: To know the cause why music was ordained.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Was it not to refresh the mind of man

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: After his studies or his usual pain?

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Then give me leave to read philosophy,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: And, while I pause, serve in your harmony.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Sirrah, I will not bear these braves of thine.

speaker: BIANCA
text: Why, gentlemen, you do me double wrong

speaker: BIANCA
text: To strive for that which resteth in my choice.

speaker: BIANCA
text: I am no breeching scholar in the schools.

speaker: BIANCA
text: I’ll not be tied to hours, nor ’pointed times,

speaker: BIANCA
text: But learn my lessons as I please myself.

speaker: BIANCA
text: And, to cut off all strife, here sit we down.

speaker: BIANCA
text:
To
                 
                Hortensio
                .
                 
              Take you your instrument, play you the whiles;

speaker: BIANCA
text: His lecture will be done ere you have tuned.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: You’ll leave his lecture when I am in tune?

speaker: LUCENTIO
text:
That will be never.
                 
                To
                 
                Hortensio
                .
               Tune your instrument.

speaker: BIANCA
text: Where left we last?

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Here, madam:

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Hic ibat Simois, hic est Sigeia tellus,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Hic steterat Priami regia celsa senis.

speaker: BIANCA
text: Conster them.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Hic ibat, as I told you before, Simois, I am

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Lucentio, hic est, son unto Vincentio of Pisa,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Sigeia tellus, disguised thus to get your love, Hic

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: steterat, and that “Lucentio” that comes a-wooing,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Priami, is my man Tranio, regia, bearing my port,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: celsa senis, that we might beguile the old pantaloon.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Madam, my instrument’s in

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: tune.

speaker: BIANCA
text:
Let’s hear.
                 
                He
                 
                plays
                .
               Oh fie, the treble jars!

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Spit in the hole, man, and tune

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: again.

speaker: BIANCA
text: Now let me see if I can conster it. Hic ibat

speaker: BIANCA
text: Simois, I know you not; hic est Sigeia tellus, I trust

speaker: BIANCA
text: you not; Hic steterat Priami, take heed he hear us

speaker: BIANCA
text: not; regia, presume not; celsa senis, despair not.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Madam, ’tis now in tune.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: All but the bass.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: The bass is right. ’Tis the base knave that jars.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              How fiery and forward our pedant is.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Now for my life the knave doth court my love!

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Pedascule, I’ll watch you better yet.

speaker: BIANCA
text: In time I may believe, yet I mistrust.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Mistrust it not, for sure Aeacides

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Was Ajax, called so from his grandfather.

speaker: BIANCA
text: I must believe my master; else, I promise you,

speaker: BIANCA
text: I should be arguing still upon that doubt.

speaker: BIANCA
text: But let it rest.—Now, Litio, to you.

speaker: BIANCA
text: Good master, take it not unkindly, pray,

speaker: BIANCA
text: That I have been thus pleasant with you both.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: You may go walk, and give me leave awhile.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: My lessons make no music in three parts.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Are you so formal, sir? Well, I must wait

speaker: LUCENTIO
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              And watch withal, for, but I be deceived,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Our fine musician groweth amorous.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Madam, before you touch the instrument,

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: To learn the order of my fingering

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: I must begin with rudiments of art,

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: To teach you gamut in a briefer sort,

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: More pleasant, pithy, and effectual

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Than hath been taught by any of my trade.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: And there it is in writing fairly drawn.

speaker: BIANCA
text: Why, I am past my gamut long ago.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Yet read the gamut of Hortensio.

speaker: BIANCA
text: “Gamut I am, the ground of all accord:

speaker: BIANCA
text: A re, to plead Hortensio’s passion;

speaker: BIANCA
text: B mi, Bianca, take him for thy lord,

speaker: BIANCA
text: C fa ut, that loves with all affection;

speaker: BIANCA
text: D sol re, one clef, two notes have I;

speaker: BIANCA
text: E la mi, show pity or I die.”

speaker: BIANCA
text: Call you this “gamut”? Tut, I like it not.

speaker: BIANCA
text: Old fashions please me best. I am not so nice

speaker: BIANCA
text: To change true rules for odd inventions.

speaker: SERVANT
text: Mistress, your father prays you leave your books

speaker: SERVANT
text: And help to dress your sister’s chamber up.

speaker: SERVANT
text: You know tomorrow is the wedding day.

speaker: BIANCA
text: Farewell, sweet masters both. I must be gone.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Faith, mistress, then I have no cause to stay.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: But I have cause to pry into this pedant.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Methinks he looks as though he were in love.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Yet if thy thoughts, Bianca, be so humble

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: To cast thy wand’ring eyes on every stale,

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Seize thee that list! If once I find thee ranging,

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Hortensio will be quit with thee by changing.
